title: judgment record — records/judgments/JDG-NNNN.json
description: One judgment, one owned file — the sign-off ledger every manual rule, deviation, risk-acceptance, and break-glass in the catalog resolves through. Field-identical to baseline-skill's judgment record (a repo running both skills shares one records/judgments/ tree; each checker evaluates the subjects it knows). Kinds: 'sign-off' (a dated human judgment satisfying a manual rule; subject = rule id) · 'deviation' (an accepted rule violation — CI-03, ENV-02, IAC-03, IAC-06, DESC-03 escapes) · 'risk-acceptance' (an accepted risk with expiry + tripwire — BR-05, FLAG-03, CI-02 escapes) · 'break-glass' (a gate bypass — HOT-04; additionally carries 'gate'). expected_state snapshots the world the judgment assumed, tripwire is the machine-evaluable condition that voids it, review_by expires it. Evaluated by `pipeline jdg check`; GOV-01 runs that evaluator in-check.

id.description: The record's identity — must match the filename.

kind.description: What species of judgment this is. A break-glass additionally carries 'gate'.

date.description: When the judgment was made. A judgment is dated or it is not a judgment.

by.description: Who judged — a person, never an agent rubber-stamp.

subject.description: What is judged: a rule id (RB-02), a scope string, or the descriptor filename.

reason.description: The rationale — why this sign-off/deviation/risk/override holds. Exists in no diff.

review_by.description: Expiry: the date by which this judgment must be re-made or it lapses. Every judgment expires — that is what keeps the ledger from fossilizing.

expected_state.description: Snapshot of the world the judgment assumed: a map of dotted fact path -> expected value (e.g. {"descriptor.profile": "critical"}). Machine-comparable by `pipeline jdg check`. Facts namespace: descriptor.* · planes.* · git.* · today.

tripwire.description: The condition that VOIDS this judgment when it becomes true — how the engine detects the accepted world changed. Date-valued comparands are computed at authoring time and stored as literals (the evaluator only compares a fact against a literal).

fact.description: Dotted derived-fact path (same namespace expected_state uses).

value.description: Comparand for eq/ne/gt/lt — any JSON value.

gate.description: break-glass only: a free-form string naming the bypassed mechanism (e.g. 'ruleset:main', 'environment:production'). Delta vs baseline's admit|reconcile enum — pipeline-skill v1 has no admit/reconcile gates (ARCHITECTURE §7.5).
